task_completion.description: Agent accessed the CAMEO Chemicals website
task_completion.visual_evidence: A web browser is open and navigated to cameochemicals.noaa.gov.

task_completion.description: Agent created the specified output text file
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal shows the creation or editing of a file named '/home/ga/Documents/hot_work_flash_point_assessment.txt'.

task_completion.description: Agent recorded the correct flash points from CAMEO Chemicals
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text file shows the following values: Acetone (0°F), Toluene (40°F), Methanol (52°F), Xylenes (81°F or 81-115°F), Acetic acid (104°F), and Ethylene glycol (232°F).

task_completion.description: Agent ranked the chemicals correctly from most to least hazardous
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text file lists the chemicals in this exact order: 1. Acetone, 2. Toluene, 3. Methanol, 4. Xylenes, 5. Acetic acid, 6. Ethylene glycol.

task_completion.description: Agent correctly classified each chemical as HIGH RISK or LOWER RISK
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the text file, Acetone, Toluene, Methanol, and Xylenes are labeled 'HIGH RISK'. Acetic acid and Ethylene glycol are labeled 'LOWER RISK'.

task_completion.description: Agent correctly calculated and recorded the HIGH RISK COUNT
task_completion.visual_evidence: The bottom of the text file displays a HIGH RISK COUNT of 4.

integrity.description: Agent actively searched for the chemicals on the CAMEO Chemicals website
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show intermediate steps of the agent typing chemical names into the CAMEO search bar and viewing individual chemical data pages, rather than relying on internal LLM knowledge.

integrity.description: Agent extracted data specifically from CAMEO Chemicals
integrity.visual_evidence: The browser tabs and active pages are on cameochemicals.noaa.gov, not general Google searches, Wikipedia, or other chemical databases.

integrity.description: Agent used the exact values from the database, avoiding common knowledge contradictions
integrity.visual_evidence: The flash point for Acetone is recorded as 0°F (the CAMEO value) and not -4°F (a common alternative value), proving the agent actually read the page.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Verified flash points from CAMEO Chemicals: Acetone (0°F), Toluene (40°F), Methanol (52°F), Xylenes (81°F or 81-115°F), Acetic acid (104°F), Ethylene glycol (232°F). Correct ranking (most to least hazardous): Acetone, Toluene, Methanol, Xylenes, Acetic acid, Ethylene glycol. Classifications: Acetone, Toluene, Methanol, and Xylenes are 'HIGH RISK' (< 100°F). Acetic acid and Ethylene glycol are 'LOWER RISK' (>= 100°F). Expected HIGH RISK COUNT is 4.
